AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Chroniques de Hainaut, MS KBR.9242.
It is associated with Jean Wauquelin's French translation of a three-volume history of the County of Hainaut.
It is associated with Jacques de Guyse.
It is associated with Philip the Good.
It is associated with Simon Marmion.
It is associated with Charles the Bold.
ConsistsOf: It consists of figures described as 'Chevaliers, conseillers, et chambellans'.
It consists of a decorative border.
It consists of the arms of the various territories ruled by Philip.
It consists of Philip's personal emblem of sparks being struck from a flint.
It consists of ten other miniatures.
CreatedAround: It was created around the time of the actual presentation to Philip in March 1448.
CreatedBy: It was created by the Early Netherlandish artist Rogier van der Weyden or his workshop.
CreatedFor: It was created for Philip the Good.
HasA: It has figures that are portraits of historical figures.
It has the arms of the various territories ruled by Philip.
It has Philip's personal emblem of sparks being struck from a flint.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by finely tailored gowns with pipe folds extending from the shoulders.
It is characterized by bodices worn over white shirts.
It is characterized by head-dresses intended to denote social position.
It is characterized by the arms, mottoes, and devices of Philip.
It is characterized by the arms of the different territories under Philip's rule.
It is characterized by concentric contour lines added to the tail of the dozing dog.
HeldIn: It was held in the Royal Library of Belgium.
It was held in the Bibliothèque royale de Belgique in Brussels.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the presentation miniature by Simon Marmion for Philip's copy of the Grandes Chroniques de France.
IsA: It is a portrait of historical figures.
KnownAs: It is known as 'Chronicles of Hainaut'.
KnownFor: It is known for being the only known manuscript miniature by van der Weyden.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Royal Library of Belgium.
It occurred in the Bibliothèque royale de Belgique in Brussels.
PartOf: It is part of the Chroniques de Hainaut, MS KBR.9242.
It is part of Jean Wauquelin's French translation of a three-volume history of the County of Hainaut.
It is part of a three-volume history of the County of Hainaut.
It is part of a book for Charles the Bold of 1468–70.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Jacques de Guyse.
UsedAs: It is used as a means to legitimise Philip's territorial claims.
UsedFor: It is used for the frontispiece to the Chroniques de Hainaut.
It is used for praising the contemporary fashion at Burgundian court.
UsedIn: It is used in the Chroniques de Hainaut, MS KBR.9242.
It is used in a book for Charles the Bold of 1468–70.
UsedTo: It is used to decorate the frontispiece to the Chroniques de Hainaut.
It is used to show Jean Wauquelin presenting his 'Chroniques de Hainaut' to Philip the Good.
WrittenBy: It was written by Jacques de Guyse.
WrittenIn: It was written in Latin by the 14th-century Franciscan historian Jacques de Guyse.
